codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of all 9 system database tables with proper SQLAlchemy models, Alembic migration, separate system DB engine/session factory, and comprehensive tests (20 passing). The architecture correctly isolates system tables from the client DB via a separate SystemBase. All columns match the feature specification. Code quality is good with proper use of indexes, foreign keys, cascade rules, and timezone-aware timestamps. Minor non-blocking style inconsistencies noted.

issues.description: Uses `str | None` Python 3.10+ type hint syntax for system_database_url while other Optional fields in the same class use `Optional[str]` from typing. Minor style inconsistency. Both work on Python 3.14.2 but mixing styles reduces readability.

BrandConfig.primary_color is String(7) which supports #RRGGBB hex only. If future requirements need RGBA or extended color formats, this length constraint would need updating. Acceptable for current PRD requirements.

Global mutable state (_system_engine, _SystemSessionLocal) is used for lazy initialization. This works for single-process deployments but could cause issues in multi-worker scenarios (e.g., with gunicorn workers). Each worker process gets its own copy though, so this is acceptable for the current deployment model.

The @compiles(JSONB, 'sqlite') decorator permanently registers a compilation rule in SQLAlchemy's global compiler registry. This could affect other test files if they need actual JSONB behavior. The module-scoped fixture mitigates this somewhat. Consider using a separate test database configuration if this becomes an issue.

No test for User-Session cascade delete behavior (deleting a user should cascade-delete their sessions). This relationship is defined in the model but not explicitly tested. The cascade would be tested indirectly when auth features exercise session cleanup.
Downgrade uses raw SQL 'DROP TYPE IF EXISTS ...' for enum cleanup. While functional, using postgresql.ENUM.drop() would be more consistent with the upgrade approach. Minor style point.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used `str | None` (PEP 604 syntax) for the new system_database_url field in config.py, while existing fields use `Optional[str]` from typing. AGENTS.md doesn't specify a preference for type hint style. Both syntaxes are valid in Python 3.14.2 but mixing reduces consistency.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: backend/app/config.py:28 uses `str | None` while lines 35-38 use `Optional[str]` for azure_openai_endpoint etc.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Pre-existing client DB models file uses deprecated `from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base` while the new system models correctly use `from sqlalchemy.orm import declarative_base`. Worker noted this as a discovered issue but correctly did not fix it (out of scope). Future workers should be aware of this deprecation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: backend/app/models/database.py:7 uses `from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base` (deprecated), backend/app/models/system.py:17 uses `from sqlalchemy.orm import declarative_base` (correct)

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker noted that the backend-worker skill's TDD approach (write tests first -> RED) doesn't fit well for database infrastructure features where the models ARE the implementation. Worker wrote models and tests together instead. The skill feedback suggests adding this exception, which is a reasonable observation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The backend-worker skill suggests writing tests first (RED), but for database infrastructure features where the models themselves ARE the implementation, writing models + tests together is more practical.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker encountered significant friction with the Create tool on paths containing spaces (OneDrive - EY path). Had to use a _tmp_writer.py workaround script to create files. This is an environment-specific quirk that other workers will likely encounter.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows multiple failed Create tool calls with 'EEXIST: file already exists, mkdir' errors, followed by workaround using Execute to write files via Python scripts.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Tests use SQLite in-memory database with a JSONB->TEXT compilation override for testing PostgreSQL-specific models without a live Neon connection. Future workers building on system DB models should reuse this test fixture pattern from test_system_db.py.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: backend/tests/test_system_db.py:30-38 — @compiles(JSONB, 'sqlite') pattern and in-memory SQLite engine fixture

summary: Feature m1-system-db-setup passes review. All 9 system tables (users, sessions, audit_log, brand_config, write_whitelist, kb_pairs, instructions, view_metadata, dashboard_metadata) are correctly modeled with appropriate column types, indexes, foreign keys, and cascade rules. The system DB engine/session factory is properly isolated from the client DB connection. Alembic is initialized with a comprehensive initial migration. 20 tests cover model CRUD, enum validation, database module functions, and the critical system/client DB isolation check. The implementation fulfills VAL-AGNOSTIC-001. Only non-blocking style issues were found (mixed type hint syntax, missing cascade delete test, no blocking defects).
